meta.title: Electrical Permit & Switching Program — 11 kV Feeder F2 to RMU-3
meta.subtitle: Switching schedule + permit-to-work (NFPA 70E Article 120 / EAWR regs 12–13)
meta.author: HSE Utilities & Power
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. A prior switching incident is cited at role level as context; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: 11 kV feeder F2 to RMU-3, ring main, Substation 2 — cable work on the feeder requiring the apparatus to be taken to a proven safe working condition (worked DEAD). The de-energization / isolation decision (below) was recorded BEFORE work. The switching sequence isolates → proves dead → earths → sanctions the work via a permit-to-work → restores; arc-rated/electrical PPE is the documented last line, never the headline control.

De-energize-first: the cable work CAN be done dead. An electrically safe work condition (NFPA 70E Article 120) is established BEFORE any work — isolate at every point of supply, prove dead, and apply protective earthing where the procedure requires it. Work is never authorised on apparatus isolated but not proven dead, or with earthing omitted where required.

sections.caption: Every point of isolation enumerated; protective earthing applied at the point of work (HV requires earthing)

sections.text: Ordered switching sequence (isolate → prove dead → earth → sanction → restore)

rows: Issue the switching schedule under control; brief the authorised person
Open feeder F2 CB + RMU-3 incomer at every point of supply; lock-off / caps-and-locks
Test for absence of voltage at the point of work; prove the unit before AND after (prove-test-prove)
Apply protective earthing at the point of work to hold the conductor at earth potential
Issue the permit-to-work (work on dead equipment) defining the safe zone, precautions, and limits
Work within the permit limits; on completion cancel the permit, remove earths, remove locks/tags, restore supply
sections.caption: The ordered switching sequence with per-step authorisation (KB-SNIP-SWITCHING-SEQUENCE)

sections.text: Sanction-to-test is kept DISTINCT from this permit-to-work. A sanction-to-test authorises controlled re-energization for testing and is never issued as a work-on-dead permit; conflating the two safety documents is a document-discipline failure.

rows.title: Protective earthing required at the point of work
rows.description: HV cable work requires protective earthing at the point of work before the permit is issued; a sequence that omits it is rejected.

rows.title: Prove-test-prove discipline mandatory before earthing
rows.description: Absence of voltage must be proven with a unit verified before and after; work is never authorised un-proven.

rows.title: Permit-to-work distinct from sanction-to-test
rows.description: This activity is work on dead equipment — a permit-to-work, not a sanction-to-test.

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (de-energize + isolate first, PPE last)

rows.control: De-energize + isolate at every point of supply and establish an ESWC (Article 120) — the primary control
Prove dead (prove-test-prove) + apply protective earthing before any work
Issue the permit-to-work (safety-document control) defining the safe zone and limits
Electrical / arc-rated PPE as the residual-hazard last line during the switching steps

rows.action: Confirm portable-earth ratings and earthing points for the F2 cable work
Verify the proving unit is in calibration before the switching operation
Brief the switching schedule and permit limits to the work party

items: Points of isolation taken from the supplied single-line diagram — a missing point of supply would be a [GAP], never an assumed isolation.
India site: state electricity-rule line-clearance return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
NFPA 70E (2024) — Article 120 (establishing + verifying an electrically safe work condition), Annex K, 120.5
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.269 (T&D — 269(d) lockout/tagout, 269(n) protective grounding, 269(m) de-energizing) + 1910.333 (work practices) + 1910.147 (LOTO)
UK EAWR 1989 regs 12–13 (means of cutting off supply + isolation / working dead) + HSG85; India CEA / state rules via hse-india
Review trigger: per switching operation / on network change / on procedure change — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (authorised / senior authorised person) must review before use.
